exercises.title: Single Group — D
exercises.hint: Look at which row pattern repeats across every column. When a variable is 1 in every cell of a group, it must appear in the simplified term.
exercises.explanation: Every minterm where D=1 is included. The K-Map shows two full rows (CD=01 and CD=11, both D=1), forming a group of 8. Only D=1 is common to all 8 cells, so the simplified expression is just D.
exercises.groupDesc: One group of 8: all cells where D=1 (rows CD=01 and CD=11)

exercises.title: Single Group — ¬A
exercises.hint: Which columns of the K-Map are entirely 1? Check what value of A those columns share.
exercises.explanation: All minterms from 0 to 7 have A=0. The K-Map shows the left two columns (AB=00 and AB=01) fully filled. These form a group of 8. Only A=0 is common to all cells, so the simplified expression is ¬A.
exercises.groupDesc: One group of 8: columns AB=00 and AB=01 (all cells where A=0)

exercises.title: Single Group — ¬D
exercises.hint: K-Maps wrap around — the top row (CD=00) and bottom row (CD=10) are adjacent. What do these rows have in common?
exercises.explanation: All even minterms have D=0. The K-Map shows the top row (CD=00) and bottom row (CD=10) fully filled. These wrap to form a group of 8. Only D=0 is common to all cells, so the simplified expression is ¬D.
exercises.groupDesc: One wrap-around group of 8: rows CD=00 and CD=10 (all cells where D=0)

exercises.title: Single Group — B
exercises.hint: Identify the two columns that are entirely filled with 1s. What value of B do those columns share?
exercises.explanation: All minterms with B=1 (columns AB=01 and AB=11) are included. These form a group of 8 in the middle two columns. Only B=1 is common to all cells, so the simplified expression is B.
exercises.groupDesc: One group of 8: columns AB=01 and AB=11 (all cells where B=1)

exercises.title: Single Group — C∧D
exercises.hint: All four minterms lie in the same row. Identify that row's CD label — both variables in the label must be 1.
exercises.explanation: Minterms 3, 7, 11 and 15 all have C=1 and D=1, sitting in row CD=11. This is one group of 4 spanning the whole row. Both C=1 and D=1 are constant, so the simplified expression is C∧D.
exercises.groupDesc: One group of 4: row CD=11 (all cells where C=1 and D=1)

exercises.title: Single Group — ¬A∧¬C
exercises.hint: The group occupies the top-left 2×2 block of the K-Map. Look at what two variables are 0 (complement) in every cell of that block.
exercises.explanation: Minterms 0, 1, 4 and 5 occupy a 2×2 block (columns AB=00 and AB=01, rows CD=00 and CD=01). A=0 across both columns, and C=0 across both rows. So the simplified expression is ¬A∧¬C.
exercises.groupDesc: One group of 4: columns AB=00–01, rows CD=00–01 (A=0, C=0)

exercises.title: Wrap-Around Group — ¬B
exercises.hint: The K-Map wraps around — the leftmost column (AB=00) and rightmost column (AB=10) are adjacent. What do they have in common?
exercises.explanation: Minterms 0–3 (column AB=00) and 8–11 (column AB=10) both have B=0. These two columns wrap around to form a group of 8. Only B=0 is constant across all cells, so the simplified expression is ¬B.
exercises.groupDesc: One wrap-around group of 8: columns AB=00 and AB=10 (B=0)

exercises.title: Single Group — B∧D
exercises.hint: All four minterms form a 2×2 block in the middle of the K-Map. Identify the two variables that equal 1 in every cell.
exercises.explanation: Minterms 5, 7, 13 and 15 form a 2×2 block at columns AB=01,11 and rows CD=01,11. Both B=1 and D=1 in every cell. So the simplified expression is B∧D.
exercises.groupDesc: One group of 4: columns AB=01–11, rows CD=01–11 (B=1, D=1)

exercises.title: Two Groups — (¬A∧D) ∨ (A∧¬B)
exercises.hint: Look for two separate rectangles. One covers 4 cells in the left two columns; another covers 4 cells in the rightmost column. Draw them separately.
exercises.explanation: Group 1: minterms 1,3,5,7 sit in columns AB=00–01, rows CD=01–11 (A=0, D=1) → ¬A∧D. Group 2: minterms 8,9,10,11 fill column AB=10 entirely (A=1, B=0) → A∧¬B. Combined: (¬A∧D) ∨ (A∧¬B).
exercises.groupDesc: Group 1 of 4: columns AB=00–01, rows CD=01–11 (¬A∧D). Group 2 of 4: column AB=10, all rows (A∧¬B)

exercises.title: Two Groups — (¬A∧¬D) ∨ (A∧C)
exercises.hint: Identify two separate groups. One wraps around the top and bottom rows (both D=0). The other is in the bottom-right 2×2 corner (both A=1 and C=1).
exercises.explanation: Group 1: minterms 0,2,4,6 sit in columns AB=00–01, wrap-around rows CD=00 and CD=10 (A=0, D=0) → ¬A∧¬D. Group 2: minterms 10,11,14,15 sit in columns AB=10–11, rows CD=10–11 (A=1, C=1) → A∧C. Combined: (¬A∧¬D) ∨ (A∧C).
exercises.groupDesc: Group 1 of 4: columns AB=00–01, wrap-around rows CD=00 and CD=10 (¬A∧¬D). Group 2 of 4: columns AB=10–11, rows CD=10–11 (A∧C)
